title: Review fix: vacuous test + guard re-elaboration Auto cell race
analysis: Review found 5 original blocking issues (1-2 resolved, 3-5 fixed in steps 1-5). Second review round found 3 additional blocking issues all related to tree-sitter-reify/build.rs robustness. These issues are pre-existing on origin/main (zero diff), but the reviewer flagged them as blocking for this branch's merge. Current state: all tests pass, clippy clean, no lint errors — the 'post_merge_verify' failure refers to the build.rs robustness concerns, not actual test failures. Issue A (resource_leak_on_clean_build): build.rs line 141 uses `unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".to_string())` as OUT_DIR fallback, which writes the grammar_hash.stamp into the source tree. Cargo always sets OUT_DIR for build scripts, so the fallback is unnecessary and creates a stamp file that escapes cargo clean. Fix: replace with `expect("OUT_DIR must be set by Cargo")`. Issue B (hard_dependency_without_graceful_degradation): parser.c is gitignored, so clean checkouts require tree-sitter CLI. build.rs panics with an opaque spawn error if CLI is absent. Fix: add a CLI availability check before generation. If CLI is missing but outputs already exist (stale stamp case), emit cargo:warning and continue with existing files. If CLI is missing and outputs are also missing, panic with clear install instructions. This provides graceful degradation without recommitting the 1.5MB parser.c. Issue C (post_merge_verify): will be resolved by fixing A and B, verified with full cargo test + clippy.

prerequisites.description: Verify tasks.json has no diff vs origin/main (issues 1+2 already resolved by prior cleanup commits 88455480/b12afa9d)

steps.description: Fix vacuous test: rewrite completion_after_dot_returns_only_members to use a defined sub-structure (like the companion test uses Bracket) and add assert!(!items.is_empty()) plus a positive assertion that at least one member variable label is returned. This makes the test fail if the implementation ever returns an empty list. Keep the existing exclusion assertions (no keywords, no builtins, no types) — they remain valid.

Verify the rewritten test passes with the current completion implementation. The DotAccess context already calls push_all_members which returns members from defined structures, so the test should pass without impl changes. If it doesn't, investigate and fix push_all_members.

Write failing test: guard_deactivate_preserves_auto_cell_value. Build a topology with a Bool param 'active' (default=true), a guarded_group whose member is an Auto cell 'x' (ValueCellKind::Auto, Type::length()), and a solver/constraint that resolves 'x' to a known value. First eval with guard=true confirms 'x' is solver-resolved. Then call edit_param to set active=false and verify the Auto cell retains its solver-resolved value (or transitions to DeterminacyState::Auto/Undef with Auto state) rather than being overwritten with (Undef, Undetermined). The test should fail against current code because the deactivation path unconditionally writes Undef.

Fix guard re-elaboration: In lib.rs, in both deactivation branches (lines ~1975-1982 for members and ~2002-2009 for else_members), add a check before writing Undef: look up the node in new_snapshot.graph.value_cells and skip (continue) if node.kind == ValueCellKind::Auto. This prevents the solver's resolved values from being silently discarded when a guard transitions to false.

Run full test suite (cargo test) and clippy (cargo clippy) to verify issue 3 (post-merge failures) is resolved. All existing guard_eval tests must still pass — the Auto-skip must not break the existing Param-based guard deactivation behavior.

Verify that build.rs line 141 currently uses unwrap_or_else fallback to '.' — confirm the defect exists by reading the file and checking the pattern. This is a code-level verification since build script internals cannot be unit-tested in isolation. Document that the fallback means grammar_hash.stamp can be written to the source tree (tree-sitter-reify/) when OUT_DIR is unset, escaping cargo clean.

Fix OUT_DIR fallback in tree-sitter-reify/build.rs line 141: replace `std::env::var("OUT_DIR").unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".to_string())` with `std::env::var("OUT_DIR").expect("OUT_DIR must be set by Cargo")`. Cargo always sets OUT_DIR for build scripts; if absent, something is deeply wrong and a panic is appropriate. Verify the project still builds after the change (cargo build -p tree-sitter-reify).

Verify that build.rs currently has no pre-check for tree-sitter CLI availability — the run_tree_sitter_generate() function spawns tree-sitter directly, producing an opaque 'Failed to spawn' error if the CLI is absent. Confirm there is no graceful degradation path when outputs already exist but the CLI is missing (stale stamp case). This is a code-level verification.

Add graceful degradation to tree-sitter-reify/build.rs: (1) Add a `tree_sitter_cli_available()` helper that probes for the CLI via `Command::new("tree-sitter").arg("--version").output()`. (2) In main(), when `needs_generate()` is true, first check CLI availability. If CLI is unavailable but all output files already exist (stale stamp, files present from prior build), emit `cargo:warning=tree-sitter CLI not found; using existing parser files (grammar.js may have changed)` and skip regeneration. If CLI is unavailable AND output files are missing, panic with a clear, actionable message: 'tree-sitter CLI is required but not found. Install via: cargo install tree-sitter-cli, or: npm install -g tree-sitter-cli'. This handles the clean-checkout-without-CLI case with a useful error instead of an opaque spawn failure.

Run full verification: cargo build --all, cargo test --all, cargo clippy --all-targets. Confirm all pass. This resolves the post_merge_verify blocker. Verify build.rs changes don't break the normal build path (tree-sitter CLI is available in the dev environment).

design_decisions.decision: Skip Auto cells in guard deactivation rather than writing (Undef, Auto)
design_decisions.rationale: The reviewer's suggested fix is to skip Auto members entirely with 'continue'. This is safer than writing (Undef, DeterminacyState::Auto) because Auto cells that were solver-resolved should retain their value — the solver's work should not be discarded. If the guard changes, the dirty cone on the next cycle will handle re-resolution properly. This matches the principle from feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md: logic errors should be noisy, not silently papered over with Undef.

design_decisions.decision: Restructure vacuous test rather than just adding is_empty check
design_decisions.rationale: Simply adding assert!(!items.is_empty()) to the existing test would make it fail permanently since Bar is undefined and push_all_members returns nothing for undefined structures. The correct fix is to change the test source to use a defined structure (like the companion test does) so both positive and negative assertions are meaningful. This makes the test actually test what its name claims: that dot-access returns only members.

design_decisions.decision: Issues 4 and 5 are out of task-208's original scope but required by review
design_decisions.rationale: Task 208 is 'Unit declarations: registry' touching reify-compiler. The vacuous test (reify-lsp) and guard race (reify-eval) are in different crates. However, the review marks them as blocking for this branch's merge, so they must be fixed here. The fixes are self-contained and low-risk.

design_decisions.decision: Use expect() instead of unwrap_or for OUT_DIR
design_decisions.rationale: Cargo unconditionally sets OUT_DIR for build scripts (documented in Cargo reference). A missing OUT_DIR indicates a broken build environment, not a recoverable situation. Falling back to '.' silently writes the stamp file into the source tree, escaping cargo clean and potentially causing stale-stamp bugs across build cycles. Panicking with a clear message is the correct behavior.

parser.c is 1.5MB of generated code. Rather than recommitting it (option a), we implement option (b): detect CLI absence before spawning, fall back to existing outputs when available, and panic with actionable install instructions when outputs are also missing. This keeps the repo lean while making the failure mode clear and recoverable.

design_decisions.decision: build.rs fixes are pre-existing on main but fixed in this branch
design_decisions.rationale: Both .gitignore and build.rs have zero diff vs origin/main — these defects were not introduced by task-208. However, the reviewer flagged them as blocking for merge. Fixing them here is appropriate: the fixes are self-contained, low-risk, and the branch will carry them into main on merge.

reuse.how: Use auto_param to create an Auto cell as a guarded-group member, and guarded_group() to wire it up with a guard expression. Existing guard_eval.rs tests (e.g. eval_guard_undef_members_indeterminate) show the exact pattern.

This companion test already uses a defined Bracket structure with known params. Reuse the same dual-structure source pattern for the vacuous test fix, combining it with the existing exclusion assertions.
Adapt the existing make_param_decl pattern to create an Auto-kind ValueCellDecl for the new test, changing kind from Param to Auto and removing default_expr.

The run_with_timeout function already handles subprocess spawning with error handling. The new tree_sitter_cli_available() helper uses a simpler Command::new().output() pattern since it only needs a boolean probe, not timeout-managed execution.
